q:
When must a driver who has broken down on a motorway or express road, stopped on the hard shoulder, wear a fluorescent safety vest?

A. Only during the day.
B. Only during the night.
C. Always when leaving the vehicle.
e:
LESSON 4
When a driver breaks down on a motorway or express road and leaves the vehicle, wearing a fluorescent safety vest is always mandatory.
This applies both during the day and at night.
clean_q:
When must a driver who has broken down on a motorway or express road, stopped on the hard shoulder, wear a fluorescent safety vest?
A. Only during the day.
B. Only during the night.
C. Always when leaving the vehicle.
clean_e:
LESSON 4
When a driver breaks down on a motorway or express road and leaves the vehicle, wearing a fluorescent safety vest is always mandatory.
This applies both during the day and at night.

q: What is the maximum speed limit (… km/hour) a car is allowed to drive on this express road with 2x3 lanes outside a built up area?
e:
LESSON 5 – ‘The express road’.
120 km/hour because the directions are separated by a berm.

q: On this express road in Flanders with 2x2 lanes, a car is allowed to drive maximum (…) km/hour.
e:
LESSON 5 – ‘The express road’.
As of 1/1/2017 in Flanders: 70 km/hour unless traffic signs or rules impose a different speed limit.
In Wallonia and Brussels-capital region: 90 km/hour.

clean_e:

As of 1/1/2017 in Flanders: 70 km/hour unless traffic signs or rules impose a different speed limit.
In Wallonia and Brussels-capital region: 90 km/hour.

q:
Who must wear a reflective safety jacket on an express road when leaving the car at a place where waiting and parking is prohibited.

A. Only the driver.
B. Driver and passengers.
C. No one does.
e:
LESSON 5 – ‘The express road’.
Only the driver. But passengers do best as well.

A. Only the driver. 
B. Driver and passengers.
C. No one does.
clean_e:
LESSON 5 – ‘The express road’.
Only the driver. But passengers do best as well.

q:
What is the maximum speed allowed for the white car on this road in the Brussels Region?

A. 50 km/h.
B. 70 km/h.
C. 90 km/h.
e:
LESSON 5
It is a normal carriageway divided into two lanes.
The maximum permitted speed in the Flemish and Brussels Regions is 70 km/h.
In the Walloon Region, it is 90 km/h.

q:
Beyond which traffic sign must you drive at least 70 km/h, if conditions allow?

A. Beyond sign 1.
B. Beyond sign 2.
C. Beyond sign 1 and 2.
e:
LESSON 5
Only on a motorway (sign 1) does a minimum speed of 70 km/h apply, if conditions allow. On an express road (sign 2), this obligation does not exist.
clean_q:
Beyond which traffic sign must you drive at least 70 km/h, if conditions allow?
A. Beyond sign 1.
B. Beyond sign 2.
C. Beyond sign 1 and 2.
clean_e:
LESSON 5
Only on a motorway (sign 1) does a minimum speed of 70 km/h apply, if conditions allow. On an express road (sign 2), this obligation does not exist.

q: When a car breaks down or with an accident on an express road the warning triangle must be placed at least (…) meters behind the vehicle so that other drivers travelling in your direction can see the triangle from a distance of 50 meters.
e:
LESSON 5 – ‘The express road’.
Motorway: 100 meters.
Express road: 30 meters.

clean_e:
LESSON 5 – ‘The express road’.
Motorway: 100 meters.
Express road: 30 meters.

q: Are you allowed to drive on an express road with a moped class B?
e:
LESSON 5 – ‘The express road’.
Motor vehicles with their trailer may on a motorway driving, except:
. Mopeds;
. Agricultural vehicles;
. Quadricycles without passenger compartment;
. Dragging fairground vehicles.
clean_q: Are you allowed to drive on an express road with a moped class B?

q:
Where must the reflective safety vest be kept?

A. In the passenger compartment.
B. In the trunk.
C. In the passenger compartment or in the trunk.
e:
LESSON 5
When a vehicle stops on a motorway or a road for motor vehicles and the driver gets out at a place where parking is prohibited, the driver must wear a reflective safety vest.
The vest must be kept inside the passenger compartment, not in the trunk, so it can be put on before exiting the vehicle.
clean_q:
Where must the reflective safety vest be kept?
A. In the passenger compartment. 
B. In the trunk.
C. In the passenger compartment or in the trunk.
clean_e:
LESSON 5
When a vehicle stops on a motorway or a road for motor vehicles and the driver gets out at a place where parking is prohibited, the driver must wear a reflective safety vest.
The vest must be kept inside the passenger compartment, not in the trunk, so it can be put on before exiting the vehicle.

q:
On a motorway, only a towing service that has been called by the police can tow a car.
What happens on an express road?

A. You may ask a friend to tow your car.
B. A towing service that you call can tow.
C. The police will request a towing service.
e:
LESSON 5 – ‘The express road’.
If your car breaks down on a highway or an express road, the police call a towing service they prefer.
clean_q:
On a motorway, only a towing service that has been called by the police can tow a car.
What happens on an express road?
A. You may ask a friend to tow your car.
B. A towing service that you call can tow.
C. The police will request a towing service.
clean_e:
LESSON 5 – ‘The express road’.
If your car breaks down on a highway or an express road, the police call a towing service they prefer.
